content.problemStatement:
You are given a Binary Search Tree (BST) and a value that needs to be inserted into this tree.
Your task is to insert the given value into the BST while maintaining all BST properties and return the resulting tree.
It is guaranteed that the value to be inserted does **not** already exist in the BST.

There may be multiple correct BST structures after insertion depending on how you traverse the tree.
Any valid BST that results from inserting the value is acceptable.

The BST will be provided in **level-order array format**, where `null` represents an empty child.

Function DeclarationFunction NameinsertIntoBSTinsertIntoBSTinsertIntoBST – Inserts a value into a Binary Search Tree (BST) while maintaining BST properties.

Parameters
rootrootroot : Pointer to the root of the Binary Search Tree.
valvalval : Integer value that needs to be inserted into the BST.
Return ValueReturns a pointer to the root of the updated BST after inserting the given value. You don't need to print anything in this question.

Constraints:
1≤T≤10001 \leq T \leq 10001≤T≤1000
0≤N≤1040 \leq N \leq 10^{4}0≤N≤104
−108≤Node.val≤108-10^{8} \leq \text{Node.val} \leq 10^{8}−108≤Node.val≤108
−108≤VAL≤108-10^{8} \leq \text{VAL} \leq 10^{8}−108≤VAL≤108
All node values in the tree are unique.
The BST is given in level-order, and -1\texttt{-1}-1 represents a null node.
It is guaranteed that VAL\text{VAL}VAL does not exist in the original BST.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^{4}104.

Input Format


The first line of input will contain a single integer **T**, denoting the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of multiple lines of input:



The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers
**N** and **VAL** —
where **N** is the number of nodes in the BST array representation and **VAL** is the value to be inserted.
The second line contains **N** space-separated elements representing the BST in **level-order**.
Use **-1** to represent a `null` child.




Output Format
For each test case, print the **level-order traversal** of the BST **after inserting** the value.
All `null` nodes must again be printed as **-1**.
Print each test case output on a new line.
constraints: All node values in the tree are unique.
The BST is given in level-order, and -1 represents a null node.
It is guaranteed that VAL does not exist in the original BST.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, denoting the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines of input. The first line contains two space-separated integers N and VAL, where N is the number of nodes in the BST array representation and VAL is the value to be inserted. The second line contains N space-separated elements representing the BST in level-order, where -1 represents a null child.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the level-order traversal of the BST after inserting the value. All null nodes must be printed as -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
First Test case: 
The value 12 is inserted as the left child of 15.
Any equivalent valid BST representation is acceptable.

Second Test case: 
9 is inserted as the left child of 13.

Explanation:
Here, 3 becomes the left child of 1.

Explanation:
Here, 10 becomes the right child of 7.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a recursive approach to insert the value into the BST while maintaining the BST properties. We start by checking if the tree is empty. If it is, we create a new root node with the given value. If the tree is not empty, we compare the value to be inserted with the root node's value. If the value is less than the root node's value, we recursively insert it into the left subtree. If the value is greater than the root node's value, we recursively insert it into the right subtree. After inserting the value, we perform a level-order traversal of the BST to print the resulting tree.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T*N) total, where N is the number of nodes in the BST and T is the number of test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the recursive call stack and the queue used for level-order traversal.
